description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a rectangular wooden cabinet, featuring a prominent convex glass screen at the front. The front panel is adorned with vertical decorative ridges and two speaker grilles flanking the screen, as well as three rotary control knobs below the display. The wood grain finish gives it a classic mid-20th-century appearance, and the brand name 'Howard' is visible above the screen. The rear of the television is open, exposing internal components such as vacuum tubes, transformers, and wiring, indicative of analog technology. One side panel has a circular ventilation or speaker port, while the other side is plain. The overall build is robust, with a hard, smooth wooden exterior and metallic internal parts.
description: A vintage wooden television set with a convex glass screen, decorative front panel, and exposed analog components at the back.
description_view_0: Side view showing a plain wooden panel with a circular black port, likely for ventilation or audio.
description_view_1: Rear view revealing the open back with visible analog electronics, including vacuum tubes and wiring.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a plain wooden surface and no visible ports or features.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the convex glass screen, three control knobs, decorative ridges, and the 'Howard' brand logo.
attribute_bezel_design: Medium-thick wooden bezel with vertical decorative ridges, brown color, and a classic mid-century style.
attribute_control_button_layout: Three rotary knobs in a row below the screen on the front panel.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
